name: 50_salience_calibration
description: Hand-labeled ground-truth tiers; run 3x to expose scoring variance. HIGH/MEDIUM boundary matters most (it gates approvals). Includes 5 generalization probes not used to tune the scoring prompt.

steps.user_message: I've decided: we're signing the office lease at $8k a month, committing for two years.
We're killing the mobile app. It's dead, effective today. All resources move to desktop.
steps.assistant_response: Mobile app sunset, everything to desktop.

steps.user_message: Cash position update: $310k in the bank as of today.
Our objective this year is to reach 500 paying customers.
steps.assistant_response: 500 paying customers — the north-star for the year.

steps.label: MEDIUM-or-HIGH: stated objective (boundary case, both valid)

steps.user_message: Priya moved from support to the QA team this week.
I generally prefer email over meetings for anything non-urgent.
The office coffee machine broke again this morning.
Traffic was terrible on the drive in today.

steps.label: LOW: small talk correctly low or filtered out

steps.user_message: We market ourselves as the friendly AI for main-street businesses.

steps.label: MEDIUM: positioning is NOT a decision (nano scored this class 0.8)

steps.user_message: We just hired a senior backend engineer — Tomás starts Monday.
We're raising the base plan from $29 to $39 per month starting next quarter.
Noticed that CompetitorX just launched their own memory feature — very basic but they're marketing it hard.
steps.assistant_response: Interesting — worth keeping an eye on.

steps.user_message: Our hosting vendor doubled their pricing without notice. Pretty frustrating.
Got a 1-star review from a customer saying onboarding was confusing. They churned the same day.
